items.prompt: computes and uses sine, cosine, and tangent to solve bounded right-triangle problems
items.expected_evidence: solve bounded right-triangle side and angle problems using trigonometric ratios
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner selects and applies the correct ratio structure for the given side-angle information.

items.prompt: explains why trigonometric ratios remain stable across similar right triangles and how side names depend on the chosen angle
items.expected_evidence: explain ratio stability using similar right triangles
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains trigonometric ratios as structural relationships, not just calculator inputs.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to side_reference_drift.
items.expected_evidence: identify correct side-angle relationships for sine, cosine, and tangent
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where the peer mislabeled opposite, adjacent, or hypotenuse relative to the chosen angle.

items.prompt: uses trigonometric ratios in a less-familiar geometric or contextual setting and checks reasonableness of the result
items.expected_evidence: verify that chosen ratios and resulting measures are reasonable for the given diagram or context
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves correct ratio structure and checks whether the computed side or angle makes sense.
